tasks.label: scan-person-emails
args:
import asyncio,re; from test1 import run_crawl; urls=['https://wise.com','https://razorpay.com']; generic={'info','support','help','hello','contact','admin','sales','careers','career','jobs','privacy','legal','security','press','media','team','billing','finance','complaints','compliance','hr','marketing','partnerships','partners','noreply','no-reply','donotreply','investor','investors','feedback','office','enquiry','inquiries','query','queries','api'}; clean=lambda e: re.sub(r'^(u003e|gt|lt)+','',e.strip().lower(),flags=re.I); personal=lambda e: ((('.' in e.split('@',1)[0]) or ('_' in e.split('@',1)[0]) or ('-' in e.split('@',1)[0])) and e.split('@',1)[0] not in generic); async def m():
  
  for u in urls:
    print(f'\n=== {u} ===', flush=True)
    em=sorted({clean(x) for x in await asyncio.wait_for(run_crawl(u),timeout=180) if '@' in x})
    p=[e for e in em if personal(e)]
    print('personal_like_count=',len(p), flush=True)
    [print('PERSONAL:',e, flush=True) for e in p]
    if not p: print('PERSONAL: none', flush=True)
asyncio.run(m())
